title: Cold outreach email
description: Write a concise, direct cold email that gets replies without being salesy.
body:
Write a cold outreach email to a {{recipient_role}} at {{company}}.

Context:
- What I offer: {{your_offering}}
- Their likely pain point: {{pain_point}}
- What I want them to do: {{call_to_action}}

Requirements:
- Subject line under 8 words
- Email body under 120 words
- No buzzwords or filler phrases
- End with one clear question, not a statement
- Tone: direct and respectful, not salesy

Write the subject line first, then the email body.

title: Professional feedback email
description: Send constructive feedback that is specific, kind, and leads to action.
body:
Help me write a professional email to {{recipient_name}} about {{topic}}.

My feedback: {{your_feedback}}
What I want to happen: {{desired_outcome}}

Make it:
- Constructive, not critical
- Specific, not vague
- Under 150 words
- Ending with a clear next step
- Tone: collaborative and respectful

title: Meeting follow-up with action items
description: Send a clear follow-up that captures decisions and keeps everyone accountable.

title: Saying no professionally
description: Decline a request gracefully while preserving the relationship.

title: Asking for a raise or promotion
description: Make a compelling, evidence-based case for more compensation or a new title.

title: LinkedIn connection request
description: Write a personalized connection request that actually gets accepted.

title: Apology email for a mistake
description: Apologize sincerely, take ownership, and rebuild trust without over-explaining.

title: Project status update
description: Keep stakeholders informed with a clear, scannable update that respects their time.

title: Negotiating a deadline extension
description: Ask for more time in a way that shows professionalism and accountability.

title: Recommendation letter outline
description: Structure a compelling recommendation that highlights what actually matters for the role.

title: Difficult conversation script
description: Prepare what to say in a hard conversation so you stay calm and clear.

title: Escalating a complaint professionally
description: Escalate an unresolved issue firmly without burning bridges.

title: Thank someone for their help
description: Send a genuine thank you that is specific and memorable.

title: Following up after no response
description: Follow up on an unanswered email without being annoying or passive-aggressive.

title: Introducing two people via email
description: Make a warm introduction that gives both parties enough context to move forward.

title: Requesting a testimonial or review
description: Ask for a testimonial in a way that makes it easy to say yes.

title: Delegating a task clearly
description: Delegate in writing with enough context that the person can succeed independently.

title: Writing a performance review
description: Write a fair, specific performance review that is useful for the employee.

title: Announcing a policy change
description: Communicate a policy change clearly and get buy-in from your team.

title: Out of office message
description: Set an out of office that is clear, helpful, and has personality.

title: Debug this error message
description: Get a clear explanation and fix for any error message in any language.
body:
I am getting the following error. Please help me debug it.

Error message:
{{error_message}}

Language or framework: {{language_or_framework}}
What I was trying to do: {{what_you_were_trying_to_do}}

Please provide:
1. Plain English explanation of what this error means
2. The most likely cause given my context
3. Step-by-step fix
4. How to prevent this error in the future

If you need to see more code to diagnose properly, tell me what to share.

title: Code review feedback
description: Get structured, actionable code review feedback before submitting a PR.
body:
Review the following code and give me detailed feedback.

Language: {{language}}
What this code does: {{what_the_code_does}}
Specific concerns I have: {{specific_concerns}}

Please cover:
1. Correctness — does it do what it is supposed to?
2. Edge cases — what could break this?
3. Readability — is it clear to a new reader?
4. Performance — any obvious inefficiencies?
5. Security — any vulnerabilities if applicable?
6. Suggested improvements with example code where relevant

title: Explain code to a non-technical person
description: Translate technical code into plain language anyone can understand.
body:
Explain the following code in plain language that a non-technical person can understand.

Code:
{{code_snippet}}

Audience: {{audience_description}}

Requirements:
- No jargon unless you immediately explain it
- Use analogies to everyday concepts
- Explain what the code does, why it exists, and what would happen if it were not there
- Keep it under 200 words
- Use examples from the audience's domain

title: Write unit tests
description: Generate comprehensive unit tests including edge cases you might have missed.
body:
Write unit tests for the following function.

Function code:
{{function_code}}

Language: {{language}}
Edge cases to cover: {{edge_cases_to_cover}}

Requirements:
- Use the standard testing framework for this language
- Cover the happy path
- Cover all edge cases I mentioned plus any you identify
- Cover error cases and invalid inputs
- Each test should have a descriptive name explaining what it tests
- Tests should be independent and not rely on each other

title: Refactor code for readability
description: Clean up messy or complex code without changing its behavior.
body:
Refactor the following code to improve readability without changing its behavior.

Code:
{{code_snippet}}

Language: {{language}}
Specific goal: {{specific_goal}}

Focus on:
- Meaningful variable and function names
- Breaking large functions into smaller, named functions
- Removing unnecessary comments
- Consistent formatting and style
- Reducing nesting depth where possible

After showing the refactored code, briefly explain the key changes you made.

title: Technical design document
description: Draft a technical design doc that covers all the important decisions.
body:
Help me write a technical design document.

Feature name: {{feature_name}}
Problem it solves: {{problem_it_solves}}
Technical constraints: {{technical_constraints}}
Audience: {{audience}}

Sections to cover:
1. Problem statement and motivation
2. Proposed solution overview
3. Technical architecture and components
4. Data model changes if any
5. API design if applicable
6. Alternatives considered and why rejected
7. Open questions and risks
8. Success metrics

title: Convert pseudocode to working code
description: Turn your logic sketch into clean, working, production-ready code.
body:
Convert this pseudocode into working code.

Pseudocode:
{{pseudocode}}

Target language: {{target_language}}
Additional requirements: {{requirements}}

Requirements:
- Write idiomatic code for the target language
- Include error handling
- Add brief comments only where the logic is non-obvious
- If there are multiple reasonable approaches, show the cleanest one
- Ensure the code is production-ready, not just a sketch

title: Write a SQL query
description: Generate an accurate, optimized SQL query from a plain English description.
body:
Write a SQL query for the following use case.

What I want to retrieve: {{what_to_retrieve}}
Table names and schema (column names and types): {{table_names_and_schema}}
Filters or conditions: {{filters_or_conditions}}

Requirements:
- Use standard SQL unless I specify a dialect
- Include appropriate JOINs if multiple tables are involved
- Add WHERE clauses for the filters specified
- Use aliases for readability
- Use the most readable approach
- Add a brief comment explaining any non-obvious parts

title: Explain a system architecture decision
description: Communicate a technical decision clearly to engineers and non-engineers alike.
body:
Help me explain a system architecture decision.

Decision made: {{decision_made}}
Alternatives we considered: {{alternatives_considered}}
Audience: {{audience}}

Cover:
1. What we decided and a one-sentence summary of why
2. The problem we were solving
3. Why we chose this approach over the alternatives
4. Trade-offs we accepted
5. How we will know if this was the right decision

Adjust technical depth to suit the audience specified.

title: Write a bash or Python script
description: Generate a working automation script with error handling and clear structure.

title: Optimize code for performance
description: Find and fix performance bottlenecks in your code.
body:
Analyze this code for performance issues and suggest optimizations.

Code:
{{code_snippet}}

Language: {{language}}
Performance goal: {{performance_goal}}

Please:
1. Identify the most impactful performance bottlenecks
2. Explain why each one is a problem
3. Provide optimized versions with explanation
4. Estimate the improvement where possible
5. Note any trade-offs (readability, memory, etc.)

Prioritize the changes with the most impact first.

title: Write API documentation
description: Generate clear API documentation that developers can actually use.
body:
Write API documentation for the following.

Endpoint or function: {{endpoint_or_function}}
Parameters: {{parameters}}
Example request and response: {{example_request_and_response}}

Include:
- Brief description of what this endpoint does
- Request format with all parameters (type, required/optional, description)
- Response format with all fields explained
- Example request (curl or language-specific)
- Example successful response
- Common error responses and what they mean
- Any authentication requirements

title: Security review of code
description: Check your code for common security vulnerabilities before shipping.
body:
Review the following code for security vulnerabilities.

Code:
{{code_snippet}}

Language: {{language}}
Context: {{context}}

Check for:
- Injection vulnerabilities (SQL, command, XSS)
- Authentication and authorization issues
- Sensitive data exposure
- Insecure dependencies or configurations
- Input validation gaps
- Any other relevant OWASP Top 10 concerns

For each issue found: describe the vulnerability, show the affected code, explain the potential impact, and provide a fixed version.

title: Write a regex pattern
description: Generate a working, explained regex for any matching requirement.

title: Explain a technical concept simply
description: Get a clear, analogy-driven explanation of any technical concept.
body:
Explain the following technical concept in plain language.

Concept: {{concept}}
Audience: {{audience}}
Analogy domain to use if helpful: {{analogy_domain_if_any}}

Requirements:
- Start with the simplest possible explanation in one sentence
- Build up complexity gradually
- Use concrete analogies from the domain specified if given
- Give a real-world example of when and why this matters
- Avoid jargon unless you immediately define it
- End with one sentence on why someone should care

title: Write a git commit message
description: Generate a clear, conventional commit message that tells the story of your change.

title: Database schema design
description: Design a normalized database schema for any application.
body:
Design a database schema for the following application.

What the app does: {{what_the_app_does}}
Main entities: {{entities}}
Key relationships between entities: {{relationships}}

Provide:
1. Table definitions with column names, types, and constraints
2. Primary and foreign key relationships
3. Indexes recommended for likely query patterns
4. Brief explanation of any design decisions
5. Any normalization trade-offs worth noting

Use standard SQL DDL syntax unless specified otherwise.

title: Write error handling for a function
description: Add robust error handling to make your code production-ready.
body:
Add comprehensive error handling to the following function.

Function code:
{{function_code}}

Language: {{language}}
Error scenarios to handle: {{error_scenarios}}

Requirements:
- Handle all specified error scenarios
- Use the idiomatic error handling pattern for this language
- Log meaningful error messages that help with debugging
- Do not swallow errors silently
- Return or throw errors in a consistent, predictable way
- Show the full updated function with error handling added

title: Code walkthrough for onboarding
description: Create a guided walkthrough of a codebase area for a new team member.
body:
Create a code walkthrough for onboarding a new developer.

Codebase area to explain: {{codebase_area}}
Audience experience level: {{audience_experience_level}}
What to emphasize: {{what_to_emphasize}}

Structure:
1. High-level overview of what this part of the codebase does
2. The main files and what each does
3. The key patterns or conventions used in this area
4. The data flow from entry point to output
5. Common gotchas or things that surprised you
6. Where to look when something breaks

title: Tailor resume bullet for a job description
description: Rewrite your resume bullet to match the exact language of the job posting.
body:
Rewrite this resume bullet to better match the job description.

Original bullet: {{original_bullet}}
Job description excerpt: {{job_description_excerpt}}
Target role: {{target_role}}

Requirements:
- Mirror the keywords and language from the job description naturally
- Keep or strengthen the quantifiable impact
- Start with a strong action verb
- Keep it to one to two lines
- Do not fabricate accomplishments — only reframe real ones
- Make the bullet scannable by an ATS

title: Cover letter for a specific role
description: Write a cover letter that stands out by being specific, not generic.

title: Behavioral interview answer using STAR
description: Structure a compelling STAR story for any behavioral interview question.

title: Negotiate a job offer via email
description: Counter a job offer professionally with a specific, justified ask.

title: Asking for an internal referral
description: Request a referral from a contact in a way that makes it easy for them to help.

title: LinkedIn post about a career win
description: Share a career achievement on LinkedIn that is authentic and generates engagement.
body:
Write a LinkedIn post about a career achievement.

Achievement: {{achievement}}
What I learned from it: {{what_you_learned}}
Audience: {{audience}}

Requirements:
- Opening line that hooks without clickbait
- Make it personal and specific — not generic inspiration
- Include the lesson or insight that others can apply
- Invite engagement with a question at the end
- Under 200 words
- Authentic tone — not a humble brag
- No more than 3 emojis if any

title: Professional bio
description: Write a bio that is punchy, specific, and right for the platform.

title: Respond to a rejection professionally
description: Turn a rejection into a relationship with a gracious, strategic response.

title: Request an informational interview
description: Ask for a 20-minute call in a way that respects their time and gets a yes.

title: Questions to ask at the end of an interview
description: Generate smart, specific questions that impress interviewers and help you evaluate the role.

title: Explain an employment gap
description: Address a gap in your employment history confidently and positively.

title: Ask for feedback on your work
description: Request specific, useful feedback rather than vague impressions.
body:
Write a message asking for feedback on my work.

Project or deliverable: {{project_or_deliverable}}
Feedback giver: {{feedback_giver}}
Specific aspects I want feedback on: {{specific_aspects}}

Requirements:
- Ask for feedback on specific things — not 'any thoughts?'
- Make it easy to give feedback with pointed questions
- Show I am genuinely open to criticism
- Suggest a format (written, quick call, etc.)
- Under 100 words
- Grateful but not sycophantic tone

title: Introduce yourself in a new role
description: Make a strong first impression with a new team or organization.

title: Resign professionally
description: Leave gracefully and protect your professional reputation and references.
body:
Help me write a professional resignation email or letter.

Last day: {{last_day}}
Reason for leaving (if sharing): {{reason_if_sharing}}
Offer to help with transition: {{offer_to_help_transition}}

Requirements:
- Express genuine gratitude for the experience
- State last day clearly
- If sharing a reason, keep it positive and forward-looking
- Offer transition help genuinely
- Keep it brief — under 150 words
- Tone: warm, professional, and final

title: Request a mentor
description: Ask someone to mentor you in a way that shows you will respect their time.
body:
Write a message requesting a mentorship relationship.

Potential mentor: {{potential_mentor}}
What I want to learn from them specifically: {{what_you_want_to_learn}}
Time commitment I am asking for: {{time_commitment}}

Requirements:
- Explain why I specifically chose them
- Be specific about what kind of help I am looking for
- Make the time commitment minimal and clear
- Show I have done my homework — reference something specific about their career
- Under 150 words
- Make it easy to say a partial yes (e.g., one call to start)

title: Counter a lowball offer
description: Respond to an insulting offer without burning the bridge or losing the deal.

title: Summarize a document for a specific audience
description: Get a concise, audience-appropriate summary of any document.
body:
Summarize the following document for a specific audience.

Document content:
{{document_content}}

Audience: {{audience}}
Maximum length: {{max_length}}

Requirements:
- Lead with the most important finding or takeaway
- Use language and terminology appropriate for the audience
- Do not just list what the document covers — synthesize and interpret
- Omit details that are irrelevant to this audience
- If the document has contradictions or uncertainties, note them

title: Compare two options with a framework
description: Get a structured comparison that helps you make a decision.
body:
Compare these two options and help me make a decision.

Option A: {{option_a}}
Option B: {{option_b}}
Criteria that matter to me: {{criteria_that_matter}}

Provide:
1. A side-by-side comparison table across my stated criteria
2. Where each option clearly wins
3. Trade-offs that are not obvious
4. A recommendation with reasoning, acknowledging the trade-offs
5. The one thing I should know before deciding that I might not have considered

Be direct. Give me a recommendation, not just a list of pros and cons.

title: Find weaknesses in an argument
description: Steel-man then critique any argument to find its real vulnerabilities.
body:
Analyze this argument for weaknesses.

Argument: {{argument}}
Context: {{context}}
Depth of critique: {{depth_of_critique}}

Approach:
1. First, steel-man the argument — present its strongest form
2. Then identify logical fallacies or reasoning errors
3. Identify unsupported assumptions
4. Find counterexamples that undermine the conclusion
5. Identify what evidence would make this argument stronger

Be rigorous and specific. Point to exact claims in the argument.

title: Extract action items from meeting notes
description: Turn messy meeting notes into clean, assigned action items.
body:
Extract and organize action items from these meeting notes.

Meeting notes:
{{meeting_notes}}

Output format: {{output_format}}

For each action item provide:
- What needs to be done (specific and clear)
- Who is responsible (if mentioned or implied)
- Deadline (if mentioned)
- Priority (high/medium/low based on context)

Also flag:
- Decisions made that are not action items
- Open questions that were not resolved
- Dependencies between action items

title: Pressure test a business idea
description: Find the fatal flaws in your idea before you invest time and money.
body:
Pressure test this business idea.

Idea description: {{idea_description}}
Target market: {{target_market}}
What specifically to challenge: {{what_to_challenge}}

Challenge from these angles:
1. Market — is there real demand? Who are the competitors?
2. Business model — how does money actually get made?
3. Moat — why would this not be copied immediately?
4. Timing — why now? Why not already done?
5. Founder fit — what specific expertise does this require?
6. Biggest single assumption — what must be true for this to work?

Be brutal and specific. The goal is to find the fatal flaw.

title: Executive summary of a report
description: Write a crisp executive summary that busy leaders will actually read.
body:
Write an executive summary of this report.

Report content:
{{report_content}}

Audience: {{audience}}
Maximum word count: {{max_words}}

Structure:
1. The situation (1-2 sentences — why this report exists)
2. Key findings (3-5 bullet points, most important first)
3. Recommendations (2-3 specific, actionable recommendations)
4. The one thing that requires immediate attention

Be ruthlessly concise. Make every word earn its place.

title: Identify risks in a plan
description: Surface the risks in any plan before you commit resources.
body:
Identify and analyze risks in this plan.

Plan description: {{plan_description}}
Context: {{context}}
Risk categories to cover: {{risk_categories}}

For each risk provide:
- Clear description of the risk
- Likelihood (high/medium/low)
- Impact if it occurs (high/medium/low)
- Early warning signs to watch for
- Mitigation strategy

Prioritize by likelihood times impact. Be specific — 'market conditions change' is not a useful risk.

title: Pros and cons breakdown
description: Get a balanced, structured pros and cons analysis to inform a decision.

title: Research paper summary for general audience
description: Translate dense academic research into clear, useful insights.
body:
Summarize this research paper for a general audience.

Paper content or abstract:
{{paper_content_or_abstract}}

Audience: {{audience}}
Key questions to answer: {{key_questions}}

Cover:
1. What question this research was trying to answer
2. How they studied it (briefly — methodology in plain language)
3. What they found
4. What this means in practical terms
5. Limitations — what this research does NOT prove
6. What questions it raises for future research

No jargon without definition.

title: Surface insights from data
description: Find the meaningful patterns and implications in any dataset or data description.
body:
Analyze this data and surface the most important insights.

Data description or content:
{{data_description_or_content}}

What I am looking for: {{what_you_are_looking_for}}
Output format: {{output_format}}

Provide:
1. The single most important finding
2. 3-5 supporting insights ranked by importance
3. Surprising or counterintuitive findings
4. What the data does NOT tell us (limitations)
5. Recommended next steps or questions to investigate

Be specific. Quantify where possible.

title: First principles breakdown
description: Break any problem or topic down to its fundamental truths.
body:
Break down this topic or problem using first principles thinking.

Topic or problem: {{topic_or_problem}}
Context: {{context}}
Goal: {{goal}}

Process:
1. Identify the core question being asked
2. List the assumptions people commonly make about this
3. Challenge each assumption — which ones are actually true vs inherited belief?
4. Identify the fundamental truths that remain after removing assumptions
5. Rebuild a clearer understanding from those fundamentals
6. State what looks different when viewed from first principles

title: Devil's advocate on a decision
description: Get the strongest possible case against a decision you are leaning toward.
body:
Play devil's advocate on this decision.

Decision I am leaning toward: {{decision_you_are_leaning_toward}}
Context: {{context}}

Present the strongest possible case AGAINST this decision:
1. The best argument that this is wrong
2. What I might be overlooking or underweighting
3. What someone who strongly disagrees would say
4. The scenarios in which this decision fails
5. What I would regret if this turns out badly

Do not try to be balanced. Be a genuine advocate for the opposing view.

title: SWOT analysis
description: Create a thorough SWOT analysis for any business, product, or decision.
body:
Create a SWOT analysis.

Subject: {{subject}}
Industry or context: {{industry_or_context}}
Perspective: {{perspective}}

For each quadrant:
- Strengths: internal advantages, what we do better than competitors
- Weaknesses: internal gaps, resource constraints, vulnerabilities
- Opportunities: external trends, market gaps, timing advantages
- Threats: external risks, competitive pressure, regulatory changes

Requirements:
- Be specific — avoid generic points that could apply to any company
- Rank items within each quadrant by importance
- After the SWOT, identify the two most critical SO and WT strategies

title: Root cause analysis
description: Find the real cause of a problem, not just the symptoms.
body:
Conduct a root cause analysis of this problem.

Problem description: {{problem_description}}
Symptoms observed: {{symptoms}}
Context: {{context}}

Use the 5 Whys method:
- Start with the observed problem
- Ask 'why did this happen?' five times
- At each level, identify whether this is a root cause or symptom

Also cover:
- Contributing factors that made this worse
- Early warning signs that were missed
- Systemic issues vs one-time failures
- Recommended corrective actions at the root cause level

title: Predict second-order effects
description: Think through the non-obvious downstream consequences of a decision.
body:
Analyze the second and third-order effects of this decision or change.

Decision or change: {{decision_or_change}}
Context: {{context}}
Time horizon: {{time_horizon}}

Structure:
1. Immediate first-order effects (what happens directly)
2. Second-order effects (what happens as a result of those)
3. Third-order effects (what happens as a result of those)
4. Unintended consequences — effects that are counterintuitive or unwanted
5. Who benefits unexpectedly and who loses unexpectedly
6. What to monitor as leading indicators

title: Product launch announcement
description: Write a compelling launch announcement that drives awareness and action.

title: Social posts from blog content
description: Repurpose a blog post into platform-specific social posts that get engagement.

title: Ad copy for a product
description: Write direct-response ad copy that converts.

title: SEO blog post outline
description: Create an SEO-optimized blog outline that ranks and converts.

title: Landing page hero section
description: Write the above-the-fold copy that makes visitors stay and convert.

title: Customer case study
description: Turn customer success into a compelling story that sells.

title: Newsletter intro section
description: Hook readers in the first paragraph so they read the rest of your newsletter.

title: Five headline variations
description: Generate compelling headline options to test for any piece of content.
body:
Write 5 headline variations for this content.

Content topic: {{content_topic}}
Target reader: {{target_reader}}
Goal of the headline: {{goal}}

Write one headline each in these five styles:
1. How-to (practical and specific)
2. Number list (e.g., '7 ways to...')
3. Question (creates curiosity or addresses a pain)
4. Contrarian (challenges a common belief)
5. Outcome-focused (the transformation the reader gets)

For each, explain in one sentence why it would work for this audience.

title: YouTube video description
description: Write a YouTube description that ranks in search and drives action.

title: Email subject line split test
description: Generate subject lines optimized for open rates to A/B test.
body:
Write email subject lines for A/B testing.

Email content summary: {{email_content_summary}}
Audience: {{audience}}
Primary goal: {{primary_goal}}

Write 6 subject lines that test different approaches:
1. Curiosity gap — creates intrigue without being clickbait
2. Direct benefit — states clearly what they get
3. Social proof — implies others are doing this
4. Urgency or scarcity — time or quantity limited
5. Question — makes them curious about the answer
6. Personalization angle — speaks directly to their situation

For each, predict the type of reader most likely to open it. Keep all under 50 characters.

title: Brand voice and tone guide
description: Define your brand's voice clearly enough that anyone can write on-brand.

title: Repurpose content across formats
description: Transform one piece of content into multiple formats without starting over.
body:
Repurpose this content into other formats.

Original content:
{{original_content}}

Source format: {{source_format}}
Target formats: {{target_formats}}

For each target format:
- Adapt the content to the natural format and length of that medium
- Identify the single most shareable insight from the original
- Rewrite for the context and audience of that format
- Do not just cut and paste — each version should feel native to its format

Preserve the core message but adapt the delivery completely.

title: Write a press release
description: Write a press release that journalists will actually read and cover.

title: Competitive differentiation statement
description: Articulate what makes you genuinely different from competitors in a way customers care about.
body:
Write a competitive differentiation statement.

My product: {{your_product}}
Key competitors: {{key_competitors}}
My unique angle: {{your_unique_angle}}

Provide:
1. A one-sentence positioning statement (internal clarity tool)
2. A customer-facing differentiation paragraph (what to say on the website)
3. How to talk about competitors without disparaging them
4. The specific situations where my product wins vs competitors
5. The honest situations where competitors might win — and how to address that

Be specific. 'Better quality' and 'more features' are not differentiation.

title: Write a re-engagement campaign
description: Win back inactive users or subscribers who have gone quiet.

title: Plan an API integration
description: Design a clean integration plan between two systems or APIs.

title: 30-60-90 day plan for a new role
description: Create a structured plan for your first three months in a new position.

title: LinkedIn About section
description: Write a LinkedIn About section that tells your story and attracts the right opportunities.

title: Portfolio project description
description: Write a compelling project description that shows your skills and thinking.

title: Script for asking about salary range
description: Find out the salary range for a role without feeling awkward about it.
body:
Write a script for asking about the salary range for a role.

Context: {{context}}
Stage of process: {{stage_of_process}}
My target range: {{your_target_range}}

Provide:
1. How to bring it up naturally in conversation
2. The exact phrasing to ask without sounding transactional
3. How to respond if they ask what I am looking for first
4. How to handle if the range is lower than expected
5. How to handle if the range is higher than expected

Keep it natural. This should not feel like a script.

title: Trend analysis and implications
description: Analyze a trend and extract the strategic implications for your context.
body:
Analyze this trend and its implications.

Trend description: {{trend_description}}
My industry or context: {{your_context}}
Time horizon to consider: {{time_horizon}}

Provide:
1. Current state - what is actually happening now vs. what is being hyped
2. Key drivers - why is this trend accelerating?
3. Who wins - which players or segments benefit most?
4. Who loses - which existing approaches or players are threatened?
5. Strategic implications for my specific context
6. Leading indicators to watch
7. Recommended actions in the next 6 months

title: Scenario planning exercise
description: Stress-test your strategy against multiple plausible futures.
body:
Help me conduct a scenario planning exercise.

Decision or strategy to test: {{decision_or_strategy}}
Key uncertainties: {{key_uncertainties}}
Time horizon: {{time_horizon}}

Create 4 distinct scenarios:
- Best case (optimistic but plausible)
- Base case (most likely)
- Worst case (pessimistic but plausible)
- Wild card (low probability but high impact)

For each scenario:
1. Name and one-sentence description
2. Key assumptions that make this scenario possible
3. How my strategy performs in this scenario
4. What actions would I regret?
5. What I should do now that works across all scenarios

title: Define a problem precisely
description: Clarify and define a vague problem so it becomes solvable.
body:
Help me define this problem precisely.

Vague problem statement: {{vague_problem}}
Context: {{context}}
Who is affected: {{who_is_affected}}

Provide:
1. Restate the problem as a clear, specific problem statement
2. Identify the root problem vs. the symptoms I described
3. What a good solution would look like (success criteria)
4. What constraints exist (time, resources, stakeholders)
5. What I am NOT trying to solve (out of scope)
6. The key question I need to answer to move forward

Make the problem small enough to be solvable and specific enough to be testable.

title: Weighted decision matrix
description: Build a weighted decision matrix to make complex multi-criteria decisions objectively.

title: Lessons learned retrospective
description: Extract the real lessons from a project or experience without blame.

title: Content calendar planning
description: Build a strategic content calendar that aligns with business goals.

title: Product positioning statement
description: Define your product's positioning using a classic, proven framework.

title: Value proposition canvas
description: Map your product's value to your customer's jobs, pains, and gains.
body:
Help me complete a value proposition canvas.

Product or service: {{product_or_service}}
Target customer profile: {{target_customer}}

Customer profile - fill in:
1. Customer jobs (functional, social, emotional) they are trying to accomplish
2. Pains (frustrations, obstacles, risks) they experience
3. Gains (desired outcomes, benefits) they want

Value map - fill in:
1. Products and services I offer
2. Pain relievers - how I specifically address their pains
3. Gain creators - how I create the gains they want

Fit analysis:
- Where is the fit strongest?
- Where are the gaps?
- What needs to change about the product or messaging?

title: Product or feature launch checklist
description: Build a comprehensive launch checklist so nothing falls through the cracks.
body:
Create a launch checklist for this product or feature.

What is launching: {{what_is_launching}}
Launch date: {{launch_date}}
Teams involved: {{teams_involved}}
Launch type: {{launch_type}}

Organize checklist by phase:

Pre-launch (4 weeks out):
- All relevant tasks for marketing, product, engineering, support, legal

Launch week:
- Day-by-day tasks with owners

Launch day:
- Hour-by-hour go-live sequence

Post-launch (first 2 weeks):
- Monitoring, follow-up, and iteration tasks

Include: owner placeholder, deadline, and done/not done status for each item.

title: Sales objection handling guide
description: Build responses to your most common sales objections.
body:
Create an objection handling guide for my product.

Product or service: {{product_or_service}}
Common objections I hear: {{common_objections}}
Ideal customer profile: {{ideal_customer}}

For each objection provide:
1. Acknowledge - validate the concern without agreeing with the objection
2. Explore - one clarifying question to understand the real concern
3. Respond - the most compelling counter with specific evidence
4. Confirm - how to check that the objection is resolved

Also include:
- The objection behind the objection (what they are really worried about)
- When this objection is actually a signal to disqualify the prospect
